measured_candidates.name: query_major_scorepage_scalar_dq
measured_candidates.read: Reached the source-boundary dQ contract but consumed dP x KV with scalar loops. This proves the binding/path but is not viable.

measured_candidates.name: query_major_scorepage_mfma_dq_threads256
measured_candidates.read: Replacing the scalar dQ consumer with CK MFMA cuts latency by about 2.2x, but it is still slower than the existing Triton full sparse-attention backward boundary before adding dKV.

measured_candidates.name: query_major_scorepage_mfma_dq_threads512
measured_candidates.read: Adding a second D-consumer wave group inside the same CTA loses to the 256-thread MFMA variant. LDS staging/thread pressure costs more than reduced serial D looping saves.

measured_candidates.name: query_major_scorepage_mfma_dq_threads1024
measured_candidates.read: Four D-consumer wave groups lose further, confirming that simply widening the CTA is not the right cooperative mechanism.

measured_candidates.name: query_major_scorepage_twostage_scaled_dp_page_dq
measured_candidates.read: A separate producer writes a BF16 scaled-dP page and a D-parallel CK/rocWMMA consumer emits dQ. This is the best dQ-only rung so far and proves D-parallel consumption helps, but it pays an HBM/L2 page boundary and still fails the 11.13 ms full sparse-attention backward gate before adding dKV.

decision.why: The best dQ-only path is now 16.29 ms with a BF16 scaled-dP page, while the current Triton full sparse-attention backward boundary is 11.13 ms including dKV. D-parallel consumption helps versus the 17.78 ms same-workgroup dQ path, but not enough to justify this two-kernel HBM-page design as a promotion target.
decision.next_kernel_direction: Keep D-parallel consumption, but do not materialize the scaled-dP page globally. The next real kernel must either use a persistent producer-consumer design with tiny immediately consumed score/scaled-dP pages or abandon full dQ materialization in this owner in favor of the faster Triton-style dQ path plus group-aware dKV epilogue. The dKV epilogue must remain group-aware before global writes; do not continue widening this CTA as the main path.

validation: S4096 MI350 timing-only scalar dQ run
S4096 MI350 timing-only MFMA dQ threads=256 run
S4096 MI350 timing-only MFMA dQ threads=512 run
S4096 MI350 timing-only MFMA dQ threads=1024 run
S4096 MI350 timing-only two-stage scaled-dP-page dQ run
